Here’s a concise summary of Cloud Adventure’s available health benefits based on the plan materials provided:

- Eligibility & effective date
  - Benefits begin on your hire date; there is no waiting period

- Employer cost‑sharing
  - Cloud Adventure pays 80% of health benefit costs; employees pay the remaining 20%

- Medical plans
  - Multiple medical options, including UnitedHealthcare (UHC) plans and Kaiser Permanente HMO options in certain regions
  - An IRS‑qualified High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP) is offered with HSA eligibility; the company makes an annual HSA contribution at the beginning of each year

- Prescription drug coverage
  - Not specified in the provided materials

- Preventive care
  - Not specified in the provided materials

- Mental health and EAP
  - Employee Assistance Program (EAP): 10 counseling sessions per person, per concern, per year (available to employees, family members, and certain household non‑dependents)
  - Additional mental‑health services are covered through the medical plans

- Telemedicine / virtual care
  - Amwell telehealth available (video/phone/chat) for clinicians including physicians, therapists, nutritionists, and lactation consultants

- Dental
  - Two dental plans:
    - Dental Plan 1: fully subsidized for employees and eligible dependents; covers preventive, basic, and major services
    - Dental Plan 2: higher reimbursement for major services and includes orthodontia for adults and children; employees share cost via payroll deductions

- Vision
  - Vision coverage is offered; specific benefits not detailed in the materials

- Dependent coverage
  - Dependent coverage is available; specifics (eligibility tiers and costs) are not detailed

- Cost‑sharing specifics (premiums, deductibles, copays, coinsurance)
  - Employer covers 80% generally; specific employee premium amounts, deductible levels, copays, and coinsurance rates are not provided

- In‑network vs out‑of‑network rules
  - Not specified in the provided materials

- Enrollment deadlines and qualifying life events (QLE)
  - Not specified in the provided materials

If you need exact premium amounts, deductibles, copays/coinsurance, prescription formularies, network provider lists, or enrollment windows/QLE procedures, please check the HR/Benefits portal or contact the benefits team for plan booklets and the full plan details.

Here’s a concise summary of the employee benefits available at Cloud Adventure, with key eligibility and enrollment notes and next steps

Health & well‑being
- Medical: multiple plan options including a high‑deductible HSA‑eligible plan. Cloud Adventure pays ~80% of health costs on average
- HSA: employer contributes at the start of each calendar year
- Core benefits generally begin on hire date (some plan exceptions may apply)
- Telehealth (Amwell), Included Health personal care assistant, Stanford Health Navigators, and CareCounsel advocacy
- Gender‑affirming care covered (hormone therapy, surgery, behavioral therapy)
- Dental: two plans, including a fully subsidized option for employees and eligible dependents
- EAP and broad wellness programs

Financial & protection
- 401(k): pre‑tax, after‑tax, and Roth options; employer match = 50% of the first 6% of employee contributions (subject to limits)
- ESPP (discounted stock purchase), FSAs (health and dependent care)
- Short‑ and long‑term disability; life & AD&D coverage (employer‑subsidized equal to 2× base compensation)
- Long‑term care insurance, business travel accident coverage
- Financial planning resources (Edelman/Ayco), legal insurance, student loan refinancing via SoFi

Family support
- Adoption assistance (reimbursement up to $14,400 per qualified adoption) and surrogacy assistance
- Fertility/reproductive resources (Inflection), Milk Stork for travel, Wellthy and RethinkCare caregiving/parenting support
- Childcare resources and discounts (e.g., KinderCare tuition discount)

Lifestyle & extras
- Commuter pre‑tax benefits, education reimbursement (up to $5,250/year for work‑related courses)
- Wellhub/Gympass fitness and wellbeing resources
- Group‑rate home, auto, and pet insurance programs

Time off & leaves
- Paid sick leave, 11 paid holidays, winter break (U.S. operations closed between Christmas and New Year’s)
- Vacation options: flexible or accrued depending on role
- Leaves: maternity, parental, caregiver, bereavement, personal, military, jury duty
- FMLA: up to 12 weeks unpaid in a 12‑month period; company provides a 50% pay benefit plus an income supplement per policy

Eligibility, enrollment windows & compliance (key points)
- Eligibility, cost, and enrollment windows vary by benefit and employee status (full‑time vs part‑time, hourly vs salaried)
- Many core benefits start on hire date; some items have specific timing (e.g., employer HSA contribution at the start of the calendar year)
- Plan‑level enrollment deadlines, effective dates, and any waiting periods are specified in each plan document
- Compliance (FMLA, leave documentation, ADA accommodations, adoption/surrogacy paperwork) must be followed to maintain eligibility

Next steps
- Review the full Cloud Adventure benefits handbook for plan‑level details, exact enrollment windows, and cost information
- Contact HR or the benefits administrator for eligibility confirmation, precise deadlines, required forms, and any questions about coverage or claims

If you’d like, I can pull out details for a specific benefit (e.g., 401(k) rules, HSA limits, or leave documentation) — which one should I summarize next?

Here’s a concise summary of the dental benefits offered

- Plans offered
  - Two dental plan options: Dental Plan 1 and Dental Plan 2

- Dental Plan 1
  - Fully employer‑subsidized for employees and eligible dependents
  - Covers preventive, basic, and major services

- Dental Plan 2
  - Employee shares cost via payroll deductions
  - Higher reimbursement for major services than Plan 1
  - Includes orthodontia coverage for adults and children

- Eligibility & effective date
  - Coverage begins on your hire date (no waiting period)

- What’s not specified here
  - Exact premium amounts or payroll deductions for Plan 2
  - Annual maximums, deductibles, copays/coinsurance
  - Any waiting periods for specific services
  - In‑network vs out‑of‑network rules and provider lists
  - Enrollment deadlines or qualifying life‑event procedures

If you’d like, I can help you request the plan booklets or point you to the benefits portal/HR so you can get exact premium and coverage numbers

Yes -  Cloud Adventure offers FMLA benefits. Key company-specific details from the benefits materials are:
Eligibility
- Employed at least 12 months
- At least 1,250 hours worked in the 12 months before the leave
Length of leave
- Up to 12 weeks in any 12-month period
Pay during leave
- FMLA itself is unpaid, but the company provides 50% of regular pay during FMLA leave
- Employees may also use accrued vacation to supplement pay
Job protection / return to work
- The documents do not include explicit company language on job-protection, but materials imply standard FMLA return-to-work protections apply

Items not specified in the materials and that you should confirm with HR
- Rules for intermittent FMLA leave
- Required documentation or medical certification processes
- How FMLA coordinates with short-term disability or other paid leaves
- HR contact or the formal claim/notification process

Would you like me to (a) pull the full FMLA policy text for more detail, or (b) submit an FMLA absence for you now (if so, provide start and end dates in YYYY-MM-DD format)?

column1: I would like to create a leave of absence
column2:
I can submit your FMLA leave. I also see one saved (not yet approved) FMLA requests on your record:
- 2025-12-10 to 2025-12-12 — 3 Days (submitted 2026-01-21)

Please tell me which action you’d like:
1) Submit a new FMLA request — reply with Start date and End date in YYYY-MM-DD format  
2) Submit one of the saved requests above — tell me which date range to submit  
3) Don’t submit yet — ask a question or request more information

Reminder: FMLA leave is unpaid under federal law; Cloud Adventure provides a company pay benefit equal to 50% of regular pay plus an income supplement per company policy. FMLA requests must meet FMLA criteria and follow the company’s documentation and timing rules

Which would you like to do next?

column1: I am ready to create a leave of absence
column2:
I show no recorded FMLA absences for the current year. If you want me to submit an FMLA absence now, please provide the required dates only:
Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)
End date (YYYY-MM-DD)
Example: 2026-03-01 to 2026-03-15 Once you send those dates I will submit the absence and confirm the submission. If you’d like more details about the company’s FMLA policy before submitting, tell me and I can retrieve the full policy text for you.
